subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board
- **Main board** — Shows 18 numbered advisor spaces (1-18) arranged in a row, plus a King's Envoy track and season track.

#### Per Player
- **Player board** — Personal building board with 20 building slots organized in 5 rows.
- **Dice** — 3 standard dice in player color.
- **Tokens** — Resource cubes (gold, wood, stone) and building markers.
- **Disc** — 1 scoring disc.

#### Shared Components
- **Year track** — Tracks 5 years of gameplay.
- **Season track** — 4 seasons per year (Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter).
- **King's Envoy token** — Awarded to the player who rolls lowest.
- **King's reinforcement tokens** — +1 military strength tokens.
- **Enemy cards** — 25 cards (5 per year), revealed in Winter for battle.
- **Resource tokens** — Gold, wood, and stone cubes.
- **Plus-two token** — White bonus die.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Central Board Setup
1. **Place the board** — Set the main board in the center showing all 18 advisors.
2. **Season marker** — Place on Spring of Year 1.
3. **Enemy deck** — Shuffle enemy cards. Deal 1 face down on each of the 5 Winter spaces (one per year). Return remaining cards to the box.
4. **Resource supply** — Place gold, wood, and stone cubes in general supply piles.
5. **King's Envoy** — Place near the board for the lowest roller bonus.

#### Advisor Layout (numbered 1-18)
- Advisors provide escalating rewards. Lower numbers give modest resources; higher numbers give better combinations of resources, military, and VP.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Player Setup
1. **Player board** — Take 1 building board. It shows 20 buildings in 5 rows, each with a cost and benefit.
2. **Dice** — Take 3 dice in your player color.
3. **Starting resources** — Begin with 0 resources (no gold, wood, or stone).
4. **Scoring disc** — Place on 0 on the score track.
5. **Buildings** — No buildings constructed at start.

#### Building Board Rows
- **Row 1** — Basic buildings (cheap, modest VP and abilities).
- **Row 2** — Intermediate buildings.
- **Row 3** — Advanced buildings.
- **Row 4** — Superior buildings.
- **Row 5** — Elite buildings (expensive, powerful VP and military).
- Buildings in lower rows must generally be built before those in higher rows within the same column.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game Structure
- **5 years** — Each year has 4 seasons: Spring (productive), Summer (productive), Autumn (productive), Winter (battle).
- **15 productive seasons** — Dice are placed on advisors during Spring, Summer, and Autumn.
- **5 Winters** — Battles occur every Winter.

#### First Player
- All players roll dice simultaneously. The player with the lowest total goes first in the first season.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Productive Season (Spring, Summer, Autumn)
1. **Roll dice** — All players roll their 3 dice simultaneously.
2. **Determine order** — The player with the lowest total goes first. Ties broken by fewest dice used (then by previous turn order). The lowest roller also receives the King's Envoy.
3. **Place dice on advisors** — In turn order, each player places 1 or more dice on an advisor space. The dice total must equal the advisor's number. Each advisor can only be claimed by 1 player per season.
4. **Collect rewards** — After all players have placed, each player collects resources/benefits from their chosen advisors (in ascending order).
5. **Build (optional)** — Each player may construct 1 building by paying its resource cost.

#### Dice Placement Rules
- You may use 1, 2, or 3 dice to influence a single advisor (the sum must equal the advisor number).
- Each advisor can hold only 1 player's dice.
- You may influence multiple advisors in a single season if you have dice remaining.
- You must place all 3 dice each season (you cannot hold dice back).

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Advisor Rewards (examples)
- **Advisor 1 (Jester)** — 1 gold. If no other player chose the Jester, gain 1 VP.
- **Advisor 2 (Squire)** — 1 wood.
- **Advisor 3 (Architect)** — 1 wood OR 1 gold.
- **Advisor 4 (Merchant)** — 1 wood + 1 gold.
- **Advisor 5 (Sergeant)** — 1 stone OR +1 military.
- **Advisor 6 (Alchemist)** — Transmute resources (trade any 2 for any 1) plus 1 gold.
- **Advisor 7-10** — Increasingly valuable combinations of wood, stone, gold.
- **Advisor 11-14** — Multiple resources plus military strength or VP.
- **Advisor 15-17** — Large resource hauls plus VP.
- **Advisor 18 (Queen)** — 2 gold + 2 wood + 2 stone + 1 VP.

#### Building
- After collecting resources, you may build 1 building from your player board by paying its cost.
- Buildings provide: VP, military strength, resource production, and/or special abilities.
- Build from bottom rows upward within each column. Row 1 must be built before Row 2, etc.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### King's Envoy
- Awarded to the lowest roller each season.
- **Benefit** — The King's Envoy lets you build an extra building during the build phase OR lets you influence an advisor already claimed by another player (share a space).
- Powerful for catch-up.

#### Winter Battle
- At the end of each year, reveal the enemy card for that Winter.
- Each enemy has a strength value (varies by year, getting harder).
- **Your military** = base military from buildings + any King's reinforcement tokens + dice roll bonus.
- **Win** — If your military meets or exceeds the enemy strength, gain the victory reward (VP, resources).
- **Lose** — If below, suffer a penalty (lose buildings, resources, or VP).
- **Tie** — No reward, no penalty.

#### Plus-Two Token
- Some advisors or buildings grant the +2 token, which adds a virtual +2 to one die in a future season.

#### Market Bonus
- Certain building abilities grant free resources at the start of specific seasons.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- The game ends after the Winter battle of Year 5.

#### Final Scoring
- **Building VP** — VP printed on each constructed building.
- **In-game VP** — VP accumulated during the game from advisors, battles won, and special effects.
- **Remaining resources** — Every 3 resources (any combination) = 1 VP.
- **No negative VP** — Score cannot go below 0.

#### Winner
- The player with the most VP wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — Most total resources remaining. Then most buildings constructed.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Game
- **Build cheap buildings fast** — Row 1 buildings are inexpensive and unlock access to higher rows. The Statue (Row 1) gives +1 VP per season and compounds over 5 years.
- **Balance resources** — Buildings require combinations of wood, stone, and gold. Avoid hoarding one type.
- **Military investment** — Year 1 enemies are weak, but they escalate fast. Start building military by Year 2 at the latest.
- **Low rolls are not bad** — Going first and getting the King's Envoy can be more valuable than high advisor rewards.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Ignoring Winter** — Losing battles in Years 3-5 destroys buildings you spent the whole game constructing. Military matters.
- **Always chasing high advisors** — High-numbered advisors require all 3 dice, meaning you can only visit 1 advisor that season. Two mid-range advisors often yield more total resources.
- **Not building every season** — Unused resources are wasted tempo. Build something every productive season if possible.
- **Neglecting building abilities** — Some buildings grant ongoing bonuses (extra resources, rerolls, military). These compound over the remaining years.
- **Forgetting advisor order resolution** — Advisors resolve in ascending numerical order. Plan accordingly.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Critical Choices
- **1 big advisor vs. 2-3 small** — Placing all 3 dice on Advisor 15 (for example) gets a huge reward, but splitting dice across Advisors 5, 4, and 3 might give more total resources.
- **King's Envoy usage** — Use it to build an extra building (especially valuable in early years) or to share an advisor space that was taken.
- **Military vs. economy** — Over-investing in military wastes VP from buildings. Under-investing risks losing buildings in Winter.
- **Building path** — Each column requires building from bottom to top. Plan your column progression to reach high-VP buildings by Year 4-5.
- **Race for contested advisors** — If you know an opponent needs a specific advisor, consider blocking them even if the reward is suboptimal for you.
